days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Landing in Japan's electric capital

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita Airport
timeline.detail: Land at Narita International Airport Terminal 1 or 2. Proceed to baggage claim and customs.

timeline.title: Narita Express (N'EX) to Tokyo
timeline.detail: Take the Narita Express train directly from the airport to Shinjuku Station. Journey takes approximately 60 minutes with comfortable seating and luggage space.
timeline.mapQuery: Narita Express, Narita Airport, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Arrive at Shinjuku Station
timeline.detail: Disembark at Shinjuku Station. Navigate to the hotel using the station's clear signage and JR East map guides.

timeline.title: Hotel Check-in at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Check into Hotel Gracery Shinjuku and drop off luggage. Request a room with a view of the Shinjuku skyline if available.

timeline.title: Lunch at Shinjuku Ramen Alley
timeline.detail: Experience authentic Tokyo ramen at one of the tiny restaurants in this legendary narrow alley filled with local flavors and history since 1945.
timeline.mapQuery: Memory Lane (Omoide Yokocho), Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Rest and Refresh at Hotel
timeline.detail: Return to the hotel to rest from travel, shower, and refresh. Prepare for evening exploration of Shinjuku's vibrant streets.

timeline.title: Explore Anime & Gaming District
timeline.detail: Walk through Akihabara or visit anime shops and arcades near Shinjuku. Browse manga, anime figures, and experience Japanese pop culture firsthand.

timeline.title: Dinner in Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Enjoy authentic Japanese cuisine at a local restaurant. Try tempura, tonkatsu, or yakitori while absorbing the energy of Shinjuku dining scene.

timeline.title: Evening Stroll in Shinjuku Neon Streets
timeline.detail: Walk through Shinjuku's iconic neon-lit streets, exploring Kabukicho, taking in the electric atmosphere, bright signs, and bustling nightlife of Japan's most vibrant district.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Rest
timeline.detail: Head back to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku, relax, and prepare for sleep after a full day of travel and exploration.

highlights: Evening stroll in Shinjuku's neon streets
localTips: Get Suica/Pasmo card at airport for seamless transit
Shinjuku Station is world's busiest - arrive early
Convenience stores (konbini) open 24/7 for snacks

days.title: Tokyo: Temples & Anime
days.subtitle: Spiritual sites and pop culture fusion

timeline.title: Wake up and breakfast
timeline.detail: Start your day with a Japanese breakfast at the hotel or nearby convenience store. Grab coffee and pastries to fuel up for temple visits.

timeline.title: Travel to Senso-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Take the JR Yamanote Line or Tokyo Metro to Asakusa Station. Journey takes approximately 25-30 minutes from Shinjuku.

timeline.title: Senso-ji Temple exploration
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple with its iconic red lantern and bustling Nakamise shopping street. Arrive early to avoid crowds and experience the spiritual atmosphere.

timeline.title: Lunch in Asakusa
timeline.detail: Enjoy traditional monjayaki or tempura at a local Asakusa restaurant. Sample local street food and experience authentic Tokyo dining culture.

timeline.title: Travel to Studio Ghibli Museum
timeline.detail: Take the train from Asakusa to Mitaka Station (via Shinjuku, approximately 45 minutes), then shuttle bus to the museum. Pre-booked tickets required.
timeline.mapQuery: Studio Ghibli Museum, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Studio Ghibli Museum anime pilgrimage
timeline.detail: Immerse yourself in the magical world of Studio Ghibli with original animations, character designs, and behind-the-scenes exhibits. Explore the enchanting gift shop and café.
timeline.mapQuery: Studio Ghibli Museum, Mitaka, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Travel to Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Return via shuttle bus to Mitaka Station, then train to Harajuku or Shibuya Station (30 minutes). Walk through peaceful forest path to the shrine.

timeline.title: Meiji Shrine nature sanctuary visit
timeline.detail: Walk through serene forest surroundings and explore Japan's most important Shinto shrine. Experience tranquil spiritual atmosphere before sunset.

timeline.title: Dinner in Shibuya
timeline.detail: Enjoy ramen, sushi, or kaiseki dinner at a Shibuya restaurant. Experience vibrant evening energy and modern Tokyo dining scene.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Take train from Shibuya or Harajuku back to Shinjuku Station. Short walk to hotel for evening rest and reflection on the day.

timeline.title: Evening relaxation and sleep
timeline.detail: Settle in at the hotel, review photos, and rest for the next day's adventures. Consider visiting the hotel's bath or relaxation area.

highlights: Anime pilgrimage to Studio Ghibli Museum
Meiji Shrine in Shibuya nature haven
localTips: Book Ghibli Museum tickets online weeks ahead
Visit Senso-ji early morning to avoid crowds
Takeshita Street fashion district nearby for anime merchandise

days.title: Tokyo: Food & Nature
days.subtitle: Culinary adventures and urban gardens

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Japanese breakfast at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku before heading out for the day's culinary adventures.

timeline.title: Transport to Tsukiji Outer Market
timeline.detail: Take the metro from Shinjuku Station to Tsukiji Shijo Station (approximately 15 minutes). Walk to Tsukiji Outer Market.

timeline.title: Tsukiji Outer Market Seafood Exploration
timeline.detail: Wander through the bustling market sampling fresh seafood, sushi, and local delicacies. Visit stalls for oysters, uni, and grilled scallops with passionate vendors.

timeline.title: Tsukiji Sushi Brunch
timeline.detail: Enjoy fresh sushi and seafood specialties at a local restaurant within or near Tsukiji Outer Market for an authentic morning meal.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsukiji Outer Market, Chuo Ward, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Transport to Shinjuku Gyoen National Park
timeline.detail: Take the metro back toward Shinjuku area (approximately 20 minutes). Walk or take a short taxi to Shinjuku Gyoen National Park entrance.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Park, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Shinjuku Gyoen Walking Trails
timeline.detail: Explore the 58-hectare garden featuring Japanese, English, and French landscape designs. Walk through seasonal flowers, peaceful ponds, and tree-lined paths ideal for nature lovers.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Park, Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Garden Café Break
timeline.detail: Rest at one of the park's cafés for matcha tea and light snacks while enjoying views of the manicured gardens.
timeline.mapQuery: Shinjuku Gyoen National Park, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Transport to Shibuya
timeline.detail: Take the metro from Shinjuku to Shibuya Station (approximately 5 minutes). Walk to the ramen restaurant in Shibuya.

timeline.title: Michelin-rated Ramen Dinner in Shibuya
timeline.detail: Dine at a prestigious Michelin-rated ramen restaurant, savoring perfectly crafted broth, noodles, and toppings that showcase Tokyo's culinary excellence.

timeline.title: Shibuya Exploration & Anime Hunting
timeline.detail: Explore Shibuya's vibrant streets, visit anime shops, and experience the electric energy of this cultural hub before heading back to the hotel.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
timeline.detail: Take the metro back to Shinjuku Station and return to Hotel Gracery Shinjuku for the evening. Rest and prepare for Day 4.

highlights: Shinjuku Gyoen National Park walking trails
localTips: Market best visited early 6-8am for fresh finds
Gyoen peaceful escape from urban chaos
Join food tour for authentic local recommendations

days.title: Tokyo to Kyoto Express
days.subtitle: Tokaido Shinkansen journey through Japan

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a Japanese breakfast buffet at Hotel Gracery Shinjuku before departure. Take time to pack and check out.

timeline.title: Hotel Check-out
timeline.detail: Complete checkout from Hotel Gracery Shinjuku. Store luggage if needed or prepare for transport to station.

timeline.title: Train to Tokyo Station
timeline.detail: Take the Marunouchi Line subway from Shinjuku Station to Tokyo Station. Journey takes approximately 15-20 minutes.

timeline.title: Tokaido Shinkansen Check-in
timeline.detail: Arrive at Tokyo Station and head to Shinkansen platform. Check boarding time and locate the bullet train platform for the 10:20 departure.
timeline.mapQuery: Tokyo Station Shinkansen Platform, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Tokaido Shinkansen Departure
timeline.detail: Board the famous bullet train heading to Kyoto. Enjoy the smooth 2 hour 15 minute ride with excellent Mt. Fuji views around 1 hour into the journey.
timeline.mapQuery: Tokaido Shinkansen, Tokyo to Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Mt. Fuji Viewing from Train
timeline.detail: Sit on the right side of the train to catch stunning views of Mt. Fuji framed by the landscape. Clear days offer the best photography opportunities.

timeline.title: Lunch on Shinkansen
timeline.detail: Purchase ekiben (train bento box) or refreshments from the onboard vendors. Traditional Japanese lunch options available.

timeline.title: Shinkansen Arrival in Kyoto
timeline.detail: Arrive at Kyoto Station after the 2h 15min journey. Gather belongings and exit the train, then head to the station's main concourse.

timeline.title: Transport to Hotel
timeline.detail: Take a taxi or local train to your hotel near Kyoto Station. Journey is typically 5-15 minutes depending on your accommodation.

timeline.title: Hotel Check-in
timeline.detail: Check in to your accommodation near Kyoto Station. Leave luggage and refresh before afternoon exploration of Kyoto's temples and culture.

timeline.title: Afternoon Exploration Begins
timeline.detail: Head out to explore nearby temples or the Higashiyama district. Consider visiting Kiyomizu-dera Temple or Sannenzaka historic shopping street.

timeline.title: Dinner in Kyoto
timeline.detail: Experience traditional Kyoto cuisine at a local restaurant. Try kaiseki, yudofu (hot pot), or other regional specialties. Many excellent options near Kyoto Station.
timeline.mapQuery: Restaurant District, Kyoto Station, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Return to Hotel & Rest
timeline.detail: Head back to hotel for evening relaxation. Rest after a full day of travel and exploration in preparation for Day 5's temple visits.

highlights: Bullet train experience Mt. Fuji views
Check-in to hotel near Kyoto Station
localTips: Board from Tokyo Station (south exit for Shinkansen)
Reserve seats in advance via JR Pass or online
Buy ekiben (train box lunch) before departure

days.title: Kyoto: Temple Treasures
days.subtitle: Ancient capitals and spiritual sanctuaries

timeline.title: Wake up and breakfast
timeline.detail: Start your day with a traditional Japanese breakfast at the hotel. Enjoy miso soup, rice, grilled fish, and pickled vegetables to fuel up for temple exploration.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Train to Fushimi Inari
timeline.detail: Take the JR Nara Line from Kyoto Station to Inari Station (about 5 minutes). Early arrival helps avoid crowds at this iconic shrine.

timeline.title: Fushimi Inari Shrine exploration
timeline.detail: Explore the famous 10,000 vermillion torii gates winding up the mountain. Take photos, experience the spiritual energy, and hike to quieter upper sections for peaceful contemplation.

timeline.title: Local snack at shrine vicinity
timeline.detail: Enjoy local street food like fried inari (tofu pockets), dango, or matcha near the shrine entrance. Several vendors operate near the base.

timeline.title: Train to Kinkaku-ji
timeline.detail: Return to Kyoto Station and take bus 101 or 59 directly to Kinkaku-ji (about 40 minutes). The scenic bus ride showcases Kyoto's neighborhoods.

timeline.title: Kinkaku-ji Golden Pavilion meditation
timeline.detail: Wander through the peaceful gardens surrounding the stunning gold-leafed temple reflected in the mirror pond. Practice meditation by the water for spiritual rejuvenation.

timeline.title: Lunch at local restaurant
timeline.detail: Enjoy kaiseki (traditional multi-course meal) or yudofu (hot pot with tofu) at a restaurant near Kinkaku-ji. Savor authentic Kyoto cuisine.

timeline.title: Bus to Ryoan-ji Temple
timeline.detail: Take bus 59 from Kinkaku-ji area to Ryoan-ji (about 20 minutes). This UNESCO site hosts Japan's most famous Zen rock garden.

timeline.title: Ryoan-ji Zen rock garden contemplation
timeline.detail: Sit before the 15-stone rock garden and meditate on its minimalist beauty. Each viewer discovers different patterns—a profound Zen experience in complete silence.

timeline.title: Return to hotel
timeline.detail: Take bus back to central Kyoto and walk or taxi to Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo. Rest after a spiritually enriching day.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner at local izakaya
timeline.detail: Experience casual Kyoto dining at a nearby izakaya. Order yakitori, gyoza, and local sake to reflect on the day's temple experiences.

timeline.title: Evening reflection and rest
timeline.detail: Return to hotel, take a relaxing bath, journal about spiritual moments, and prepare for Day 6. Early sleep recommended.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

localTips: Fushimi Inari hike takes 1.5-2 hours round trip
Arrive at temples early before tour groups
Buy combination temple pass for discounts

days.title: Kyoto: Geisha & Nature
days.subtitle: Traditional culture meets bamboo forests

timeline.title: Hotel breakfast
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Japanese breakfast at Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo before heading out for the day.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Transport to Arashiyama
timeline.detail: Take a train from Kawaramachi Station to Arashiyama Station (approximately 15 minutes). Early arrival ensures fewer crowds at the bamboo grove.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawaramachi Station to Arashiyama Station, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove sacred walk
timeline.detail: Experience the serene bamboo forest with towering stalks creating natural cathedral-like passages. Visit early to enjoy peaceful atmospheres before tourist crowds arrive.

timeline.title: Tenryu-ji Temple and garden
timeline.detail: Explore one of Kyoto's most important temples with its stunning traditional garden featuring borrowed scenery of the Arashiyama mountains. Walk through the temple grounds and admire the carefully designed landscape.

timeline.title: Lunch near Arashiyama
timeline.detail: Enjoy local Kyoto cuisine at a riverside restaurant in Arashiyama. Try yudofu (hot pot) or soba noodles with fresh local vegetables.

timeline.title: Transport to Gion district
timeline.detail: Take bus or train from Arashiyama to Gion district (approximately 30 minutes). Arrive in time to explore before evening geisha spotting.

timeline.title: Gion district exploration
timeline.detail: Walk through Kyoto's famous geisha district with traditional wooden machiya houses, narrow cobblestone streets, and historic tea houses. Explore shops and small cafes.

timeline.title: Geisha spotting at dusk
timeline.detail: Position yourself along Hanami-koji or Pontocho streets around dusk to spot geisha traveling to evening appointments. Respectful observation from a distance is customary.

timeline.title: Traditional kaiseki dinner
timeline.detail: Experience an authentic kaiseki multi-course dinner at an upscale restaurant in or near Gion. Each course showcases seasonal ingredients and meticulous presentation reflecting Japanese culinary art.

timeline.title: Return to hotel
timeline.detail: Take taxi or bus back to Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo. Reflect on the day's cultural experiences.
timeline.mapQuery: Gion to Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Evening rest
timeline.detail: Relax in your hotel room and prepare for the final day of the trip. Review photos and plan any remaining activities.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

highlights: Gion district geisha spotting at dusk
localTips: Bamboo grove magical before 7am or after 5pm
Gion tea houses book experiences in advance
Walk Philosopher's Path canal for peaceful atmosphere

days.title: Kyoto: Food & Markets
days.subtitle: Culinary immersion in ancient capital

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a traditional Japanese breakfast at Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo before heading out for the day.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Walk to Nishiki Market
timeline.detail: Take a short 10-minute walk from the hotel to Nishiki Market, Kyoto's famous food market street.

timeline.title: Nishiki Market Food Tour
timeline.detail: Explore the historic market with a guided food tour, sampling fresh seafood, pickles, sweets, and local specialties from various stall owners.
timeline.mapQuery: Nishiki Market, Chuo Ward, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Matcha Tea Ceremony Experience
timeline.detail: Participate in an authentic Japanese matcha tea ceremony, learning the traditional preparation and presentation methods in a historic setting.

timeline.title: Lunch at Local Ramen Shop
timeline.detail: Enjoy authentic Kyoto-style ramen at a traditional family-run restaurant, known for rich tonkotsu or shoyu-based broths.
timeline.mapQuery: Ramen Yokocho, Shimogyo Ward, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Rest and Explore Higashiyama District
timeline.detail: Stroll through the charming preserved wooden machiya houses and temples in the historic Higashiyama district near Kiyomizu-dera.

timeline.title: Tonkatsu Dinner
timeline.detail: Savor perfectly breaded and fried pork cutlet at a celebrated local tonkatsu restaurant with crispy exterior and tender meat.

timeline.title: Evening Temple Illumination at Kiyomizu-dera
timeline.detail: Visit the magnificent Kiyomizu-dera temple during evening illumination hours to experience the stunning wooden architecture bathed in atmospheric light.

timeline.title: Return to Hotel and Evening Stroll
timeline.detail: Walk back to Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, enjoying the quiet evening atmosphere of Kyoto's illuminated streets.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Rest at Hotel
timeline.detail: Return to your hotel room to relax and prepare for the next day of your cultural adventure.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

highlights: Nishiki Market food tour and tastings
localTips: Nishiki Market vendors speak limited English - use translator app
Tea ceremony reservations required in advance
Summer festival season - check for local matsuri events

days.title: Departure from Kyoto
days.subtitle: Final memories and journey home

timeline.title: Wake up and breakfast
timeline.detail: Enjoy a final Japanese breakfast at Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo before checkout. Savor local specialties one last time.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Hotel checkout
timeline.detail: Complete checkout procedures at Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo. Store luggage with concierge if early departure needed.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel Gracery Kyoto Sanjo, Kyoto, Japan

timeline.title: Morning temple visit - Fushimi Inari
timeline.detail: Visit the iconic Fushimi Inari Taisha with its thousands of vermillion torii gates for stunning final memories and photography.

timeline.title: Last-minute shopping
timeline.detail: Browse Kyoto's traditional shops near Sanjo area for final souvenirs, matcha tea, or anime merchandise before departure.

timeline.title: Lunch in Kyoto
timeline.detail: Enjoy a final traditional Kyoto meal such as kaiseki, ramen, or local udon at a restaurant near Kyoto Station.

timeline.title: Travel to Kyoto Station
timeline.detail: Take local train (Karasuma Line) or taxi from Hotel Gracery to Kyoto Station with collected luggage. Journey takes 15-20 minutes.

timeline.title: Haruka Express train check-in
timeline.detail: Check in for the Haruka Express train to Kansai International Airport. Secure seats and board for the scenic 75-minute journey.

timeline.title: Haruka Express to Kansai Airport
timeline.detail: Ride the express train from Kyoto to Kansai International Airport with comfortable seating and station announcements.

timeline.title: Airport arrival and check-in
timeline.detail: Arrive at Kansai International Airport. Check in baggage, complete immigration procedures, and proceed to departures.

timeline.title: Airport dining and relaxation
timeline.detail: Explore duty-free shops or enjoy a final meal at airport restaurants before boarding your international flight home.

highlights: Check-out and transit to Kyoto Station
Flight home via Kansai International Airport
localTips: Kyoto Station to KIX Airport: 75 min by Haruka Express
Allow 3 hours before international flight
Last-minute souvenir shopping in station mall

hotels.name: Hotel Gracery Shinjuku
hotels.note: 2-min walk from Shinjuku Station East Exit

budget.note: Return flight 2 travelers via MH or JAL, booked 2+ months in advance
Not needed for this route, local IC cards sufficient
Tokyo metro and Kyoto bus systems, ~$50 per traveler
Mix of street food, ramen, and 1-2 upscale meals, ~$350 per traveler
Anime merchandise, matcha, crafts, local goods
Narita Express + Haruka Express round trip

urgent.label: Book Ghibli Museum tickets
urgent.note: Requires advance online booking, sells out days ahead. Only reserved entry slots available

urgent.label: Reserve Shinkansen seats
urgent.note: Book Tokyo-Kyoto round trip at least 2 weeks in advance, especially for July peak season

urgent.label: Book tea ceremony & geisha experience
urgent.note: Popular Kyoto experiences need 1-2 weeks advance booking, some require credit card guarantee

urgent.label: Purchase Suica/Pasmo IC cards
urgent.note: Can be purchased at Narita/Haneda airport upon arrival, but pre-ordering avoids queues

urgent.label: Check temple closures & hours
urgent.note: Verify Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama hours and any summer maintenance

tickets.name: Narita Express: NRT Airport → Tokyo Station
Haruka Express: Kyoto Station → KIX Airport
